title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (March 19, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Bambola and The Girl Next Door open at former Seaport Social location, offering Italian staples and street food.
Common Craft expands to South Boston with a new menu by chef Tony Messina featuring seasonal and craft dishes.
Roger’s Fish Co. opens at Logan Airport’s Terminal A, offering classic New England seafood from owner Roger Berkowitz.

title: Watch: Stephen Curry wasn’t thrilled about a fan grabbing his arm at TD Garden
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A fan at TD Garden grabbed Stephen Curry's arm during the Celtics-Warriors game, leading to the fan's ejection.
Stephen Curry missed the game against the Celtics due to knee pain; the Warriors lost 120-99 without him.
Warriors coach Steve Kerr reflected on the team's challenges, stating they lack the firepower needed to compete.

title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum show what’s possible in Celtics’ win over Warriors: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: The Boston Celtics defeated the Golden State Warriors 120-99, with strong performances from Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum.
Jaylen Brown scored 32 points and Jayson Tatum added 24, continuing their standout contributions to the Celtics' success.
Kristaps Porzingis returned to TD Garden as a Warrior, receiving a warm ovation and tribute video from Celtics fans.

title: Steve Kerr explains how the Celtics went from an ‘afterthought’ to a ‘real favorite’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Steve Kerr praised the Boston Celtics for becoming a top Eastern Conference team without star Jayson Tatum.
The Celtics demonstrated depth by sweeping the Warriors, winning decisively 120-99 at TD Garden.
Kerr highlighted the importance of the Celtics' spacing and young players' development during Tatum's absence.

title: Advocates sue Worcester over response to mental health 911 calls, alleging discrimination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Advocates filed a federal lawsuit against Worcester, alleging discrimination in handling mental health 911 calls with armed police.
The lawsuit claims Worcester's default response to mental health emergencies involves sending police, which can escalate situations.
Less than 25% of Worcester police have crisis intervention training, and the city's pilot Mental Health Crisis Response Team disbanded.

title: Hilary Duff will be Northeastern University’s 2026 commencement speaker
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Hilary Duff announced as Northeastern University's 2026 commencement speaker for their April 29 event at Fenway Park.
Duff, known for 'Lizzy Maguire' and 'A Cinderella Story,' will address graduating students.
Previous speakers include notable figures like Coretta Scott King, John F. Kennedy, and David A. Thomas.

title: 3 roster needs Patriots must still address after free-agency spending spree
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots seek a No. 1 wide receiver, potentially targeting A.J. Brown or drafting KC Concepcion.
New England aims to bolster its pass rush, considering draft prospects like Cashius Howell and Zion Young.
Depth at offensive tackle is a priority, with potential draft pick Blake Miller as a long-term solution.

title: Needham 16-year-old charged with murder in Roxbury slaying of woman, 61
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A Needham 16-year-old is charged with murder in the October fatal shooting of Marie Molea-Anthony in Roxbury.
The teen, detained on unrelated firearms charges, faces additional counts of murder, aggravated assault, and firearm possession.
Boston police continue to investigate the Roxbury shooting and request information from the public to assist with the case.

title: Chelsea man released decades after murder conviction won’t be retried, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Thomas Rosa Jr., convicted of a 1985 murder, was released after 34 years; charges have been dropped.
New forensic evidence, lost evidence, and changed case law led Suffolk DA to vacate Rosa's conviction.
Rosa is the 100th exoneration in Massachusetts since 1989, highlighting issues with eyewitness misidentification.

title: Former Attleboro man gets 35-40 years in prison for raping 5-year-old niece
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: William Machado, 35, sentenced to 35-40 years in prison for raping his 5-year-old niece in 2022.
Machado convicted of multiple charges including rape of a child by force and indecent assault.
He was apprehended in Chicago after initially evading arrest; extradited to Massachusetts for trial.
